description:
This diagram illustrates components and conceptual relationships for the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) European Ground Motion Service (EGMS) Calibrated Product.
Sub-image (a) shows a map of Europe, extending from the UK and Ireland eastward to parts of Russia and Turkey, and including North Africa's Mediterranean coast. This map is overlaid with a dense, uniform grid of red circular points covering the landmasses. Country borders are outlined in yellow. This visual likely represents the spatial coverage or a grid for measurement points processed by the EGMS.
Sub-image (b) is a system illustration depicting a SAR (Synthetic Aperture Radar) Platform satellite on the left, a GNSS (Global Navigation Satellite System) Platform satellite on the right, and a ground-based GNSS Station. The GNSS Platform is shown transmitting signals to the GNSS Station, establishing a precise spatial reference for geodetic measurements. This setup demonstrates how space-borne and ground-based GNSS components interact to provide calibration for SAR measurements.
Sub-image (c) is a conceptual diagram showing five ground stations, each represented by a tower with an antenna and its own local coordinate system (blue axes), arranged in a network. These stations are connected by dashed orange lines. At the central point of this network, a larger coordinate system (purple axes) and a green oval indicate a combined, absolute reference frame, which is likely derived from the integration of data from the network of GNSS stations. This diagram illustrates the principle of establishing a precise, anchored reference frame, such as the European Terrestrial Reference System 89 (ETRS89), for ground motion monitoring.
